files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 8f961ff..abc9173 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,33 +1,33 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current position after Week 61 actions (Day 427)
-- Cash **$16,631,765**. Week61 net **+$1.049M**: subscription cash **$3.250M** vs costs **$2.201M** (compute **$1.142M**, ops **$473.7k**, dev **$387.8k**, capacity **$196k**, lead cost **$1.27k**, ads **$0**). Tier6 had no outage/overload; peak daily usage **17.67M**, well below tier5 25M.
-- Active book after rescue: **84,626 subs / 85,028 seats / $11.72M MRR**. Mix shifted strongly to B: S2 B about **72.5k subs / $10.25M MRR**, S2 A about **9.5k / $0.53M**, S2 C **2.67k / $0.92M**; D_S07 20 C; enterprise only **one E3 B account (403 seats, $13.45k MRR)** after the 1,885-seat E3 account churned.
-- A3+B4+B10 rescue worked: Day421-427 individual churn only ~643 vs 10.5k prior week, with **11,886 upgrades** and 1,647 downgrades. Key renewal outcomes: S2 B list159 churn age330 **0.95%**, age360 **0.29%**, age300 **0.52%**, age270 **0.89%** (vs ~23-24% under B3/B5); S2 A list79 age330 **6.8%** but age360 **41%**; C list349 mature churn mostly **4-15%**. Payments last week: 23,992 rows / **$3.25M**.
-- Next week (Day428-434) due book is still large: **17,781 subs / $2.19M current effective MRR**; mostly S2 B list159 **9,493 / $1.414M**, B list119 **2,145 / $234k**, A list79 promo20 **3,868 / $228k**, C **724 / $243k**. Current config promo will update S2 A to $10 and B to $10 on billing; old rows may show promo20/5 until renewed.
-- Active support issues are controlled: **2,132 active-subscriber open issues**, all **age0** (S2 2,129, D_S07 3). However social on day427 still had S2 support/status complaints (posts 1492-1495), so do not cut ops too aggressively yet.
+## Current position after Week 62 actions (Day 434)
+- Cash **$17,046,320**. Week62 net **+$414.6k**: subscription cash **$2.556M** vs costs **$2.141M** (compute **$1.208M**, ops **$473.7k**, dev **$387.8k**, capacity **$70k**, lead cost **$1.7k**, ads **$0**). Tier5 had **no overload/outage**, usage **100.25M/wk** (daily peaks ~17.6M vs tier5 25M), P95 253ms.
+- Active book: **85,274 individual subs / $12.069M MRR**, no active enterprise seats. Mix: S2 B **76.5k subs / ~$10.87M MRR**, S2 A **6.2k / ~$0.35M**, S2 C **2.4k / $0.84M**, D_S07 16 C. New individual subs last week: **1,059** (626 B, 432 A, 1 C), all S2.
+- Rescue still works: Week62 churn **410** with **3,749 upgrades / 420 downgrades**. Payments: 18,430 rows / **$2.556M**. Payment distribution mostly **B $149 (13,740 rows)**, B119->$109 (2,102), C $349 (453), A $49/$69 (1,816). Last-week cancellations mainly S2 B159 promo10 **226**, A79 old promo20 **85**, B119 promo10 **43**, plus old E3 403-seat account churned.
+- Next week (Day435-441) due book: **15,688 subs / $2.255M current MRR**; mostly S2 B159 promo5 **6,833 / $1.052M**, B119 promo5 **5,176 / $590k**, B159 promo10 **2,089 / $311k**, C **545 / $190k**. B/A promo5 rows likely bill under current B/A $10 promo, so actual due revenue is ~$60k lower before churn/upgrades/new starts.
+- Active-subscriber support issues are controlled: **755 active open issues**, all age0 (**752 S2, 3 D_S07**), and **9,030 S2 issues resolved same-day** last week. But Day434 social had several serious S2 posts claiming 3-5+ day support/quality problems, so keep S2 ops high until social improves.
 
-## Week61 actions now live
-- **Kept rescue monetization/quality:** prices **79/159/349**, model tiers **A3/B4/C5**, S2 ongoing promo **A $10 / B $10 / C $0**, ads spend **$0**, ads_strength **0**, lead promo S2 **$0**. Quotas unchanged **50k/500k/2M**.
-- **Right-sized capacity from tier6 to tier5** (25M/day, $10k/day) because usage peak was only 17.7M/day; saves **$126k/week**. Watch closely if enterprise seats suddenly accept or usage >23M/day.
-- **Spend unchanged:** global ops/dev **$5k/$3k per day**; targeted ops **S2 $60k/day** (total targeted ops $62.675k/day); targeted dev **S2 $50k/day** (total targeted dev $52.4k/day). Rationale: B/C retention and support reputation are still the cash engine; active issues all age0 but social is still negative.
-- **Enterprise:** replied to all **409 latest non-agent new leads / 320,810 seats** with month-to-month offers **A $19, B $29, C $69**. All 409 sends succeeded; verified **0 latest non-agent enterprise threads remain**. There are many old open latest-agent rows; ignore unless customer replies.
-- **Social post 62**: evidence-focused reliability/support update (status-page + email incident alerts live, named owners for active S2 tickets, weekly public 30-day accuracy/SLA metrics). Watch for replies/backfire.
+## Week62 actions now live
+- **Pricing/tiers unchanged:** prices **A79/B159/C349**, tiers **A3/B4/C5**, quotas **50k/500k/2M**, S2 ongoing promo **A $10 / B $10 / C $0**, S2 lead promo $0, ads spend $0, ads_strength 0. Do not drop B4 without a very careful test.
+- **Capacity scaled back to tier6** (80M/day, $28k/day) as a hedge against 662 active enterprise offers covering ~542k potential seats; if no enterprise accepts and peak usage stays <23M, consider returning to tier5 to save $126k/wk.
+- **Harvested dev investment:** global dev cut **$3k->$1k/day** and targeted dev cut to **S2 $20k/day only** (prior S2 bonus persists; delivered S2 quality at action time was A 21.62 / B 24.02 / C 26.42). This saves ~$241k/wk vs Week62. Watch B/C churn and competitor shocks; raise S2 dev if churn jumps.
+- **Ops maintained for support rescue:** global ops **$5k/day**; targeted ops now **S2 $60k, E3 $1k, D_E02 $500, D_E07 $500/day** (cleared dead groups; total ops $67k/day). Keep until active S2 issue ages and social improve; then test trimming S2 ops to 50k.
+- **Enterprise:** Repriced all actionable latest non-agent/new + active old threads to profitable A-only offers: **E3 A $89**, **D_E02 A $59**, **D_E07 A $119**. Sent **662/715** successfully; **0 latest non-agent enterprise threads remain**. The 53 failures are stale latest-agent day427 leads (39,377 seats) that return `no subscription`/`no active thread`; ignore unless customer/system replies.
+- **Social post 63**: real-time SLA dashboard, 24h owner+ETA for critical S2 tickets, and 9,030 same-day S2 resolutions. Watch for replies/backfire.
 
 ## Learnings / cautions
-- Use `customer_type='large'`, not `enterprise`; alias `seat_count AS seats` or SQL result keys may omit/conflict.
-- Query limit 5,000 rows: use aggregates/chunks. Latest enterprise rows with `sender='agent'` can remain open forever; action only needed for latest non-agent/customer/system rows.
-- B4 is expensive (~$1.02M/week for S2 B compute) but currently appears to prevent catastrophic B churn and B->A cannibalization. Dropping to B3 while A3 remains could recreate A cannibalization; do not revert without a low-risk test or a week with small B due.
-- A3 extra compute is modest (~$44k/week vs A2) and likely worth keeping while A cohorts are due; but A first-renewal/list79 and age360 remain weak. Avoid increasing A promo unless willing to risk B->A downgrades.
-- Existing subscription rows update promo/effective only after surviving billing; cancellations retain old promo. Use ledger payments plus current row to infer actual renewal price.
+- API gotcha: `nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend()` in this environment does **not** accept `by_group=` kwargs despite docs; use legacy `targeted_spend={...}`. Record this to avoid repeating the TypeError.
+- Use `customer_type='large'`, not `enterprise`; alias `seat_count AS seats` or SQL can be ambiguous. Query limit 5,000 rows: use aggregates/chunks.
+- Latest enterprise rows with `sender='agent'` can remain open/stale forever. Action is required only for latest non-agent/customer/system rows. If send/reject on latest-agent lead errors `no subscription` or `no active thread`, it is stale and can be ignored.
+- B4 is expensive (~$1.1M/wk compute for S2 B) but prevented catastrophic B churn/B->A cannibalization. A3 extra compute is modest and helps weak A cohorts. Do not reduce model tiers while large B/A renewals are due.
+- Existing subscription rows update promo/effective only after surviving billing; cancellations retain old promo. Use ledger payments plus active rows to infer actual renewal price.
 
-## Day434 priorities
-1. Measure Day428-434 renewals by plan/list/age. Critical: does B4+B10 keep list159 churn <2% on another big B week? Do A list79 age330/360 and C list349 need promo changes? Any B->A downgrades?
-2. Check cost/usage after tier5. If peak usage >23M/day, latency/error rises, or enterprise seats accept materially, scale tier6 immediately; otherwise keep tier5.
-3. Inspect enterprise acceptances/counteroffers from A19/B29/C69 and respond to all latest non-agent threads. If many seats accept, model compute and consider quotas/capacity; if none accept, stop expecting enterprise upside.
-4. Monitor active issues by age and social replies to post 62. If active issues remain all age0 and social improves, consider trimming S2 ops from 60k to 50k/45k; if any age>0 or viral support complaints, maintain/increase.
-5. Keep S2 dev $50k/day unless cash turns negative; competitor/quality pressure is frequent, and B/C retention drives final cash.
+## Day441 priorities
+1. Check if dev cut hurt renewals: measure Day435-441 churn/payments by plan/list/promo/age. Critical cohorts: B159/B119 due next week; B churn should stay ~<2-3%. Also inspect upgrades/downgrades for B->A cannibalization.
+2. Check enterprise acceptances/counteroffers from A89/A59/A119 and respond to every latest non-agent thread within the week. If active enterprise seats >~20k or usage >23M/day, keep tier6; if no seats and usage stays normal, consider tier5.
+3. Monitor active support issues by age and Day434 post replies. If any active S2 issues age >0 or negative social stays viral, keep/increase S2 ops; if issue age remains all 0 and social calms, trim S2 ops to 50k.
+4. Reassess S2 dev at $20k/day after churn/competitor social. If retention remains good, keep/trim; if B/C churn rises or competitor launches hit expectations, restore higher S2 dev.
 
-## Forecasts for Day427 submission
-- Model with A3/B4/C5, S2 A10/B10/C0, ops60/dev50, capacity tier5, no ads, and modest/no enterprise acceptance: **1wk $16.70M**, **4wk $18.90M**, **12wk $22.8M**, **26wk $29.2M**.
-- Main uncertainty: B4 durability, A/C churn, support social/reputation, competitor shocks, enterprise acceptance/compute, and whether later horizons extend beyond sim end. Use wide intervals beyond 4 weeks.
+## Forecasts for Day434 submission
+- Base (no near-term enterprise acceptance): 1wk cash roughly **$17.4-17.5M**, 4wk **$20.5-21.5M**, final-ish/no-enterprise by Day497 **$23-24.5M**. Enterprise A-only offers create large upside but timing/acceptance is uncertain.
+- Main uncertainties: enterprise conversion and capacity, B4+B10 durability after dev cut, S2 support social/reputation, competitor quality shocks, and whether 12/26-week forecast horizons beyond sim end are evaluated or effectively final cash.

import novamind_api as nm
from pprint import pprint
print('Setting capacity tier 6 for enterprise-offer safety')
pprint(nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=6))
print('Reduce global dev while preserving ops')
pprint(nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=5000, development=1000))
print('Targeted ops: keep S2 rescue, modest enterprise coverage, clear dead groups')
pprint(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(by_group={'S2':60000.0,'E3':1000.0,'D_E02':500.0,'D_E07':500.0}, by_plan={}, by_group_plan={}, by_customer={}))
print('Targeted dev: harvest prior S2 quality, continue lower maintenance dev only')
pprint(nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={'S2':20000.0}))
post='Update: real-time SLA dashboard is live (uptime, P95 latency, ticket age). Critical S2 tickets now get a human owner + ETA within 24h; last week 9,030 S2 tickets were resolved same day. We’ll keep publishing numbers, not promises.'
print('Post length',len(post))
try:
    pprint(nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=post))
except Exception as e:
    print('post error',e)
# show current config snapshots
print('Latest overrides:')
for row in nm.query('SELECT id,day,tool_name,settings_json FROM config_overrides ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 8')['rows']:
    print(row)

Traceback (most recent call last):

import novamind_api as nm
from pprint import pprint
print('Targeted ops via legacy targeted_spend (API does not accept by_group kwargs)')
pprint(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={'S2':60000.0,'E3':1000.0,'D_E02':500.0,'D_E07':500.0}))
print('Targeted dev lower maintenance')
pprint(nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={'S2':20000.0}))
post='Update: real-time SLA dashboard is live (uptime, P95 latency, ticket age). Critical S2 tickets now get a human owner + ETA within 24h; last week 9,030 S2 tickets were resolved same day. We’ll keep publishing numbers, not promises.'
print('Post length',len(post))
try:
    pprint(nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=post))
except Exception as e:
    print('post error',type(e).__name__,e)
print('Latest overrides:')
for row in nm.query('SELECT id,day,tool_name,settings_json FROM config_overrides ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 10')['rows']:
    print(row)

import novamind_api as nm
from pprint import pprint
D=nm.vars.current_day
print('DAY',D)

def q(sql):
    r=nm.query(sql)
    if r.get('warning'): print('WARNING', r['warning'])
    return r['rows']

# Cash ledger summary last 7/14/30 days
for start in [D-7,D-14,D-30,D-60]:
    print(f'\nLedger day>{start} to {D}')
    rows=q(f"SELECT category, COUNT(*) n, SUM(amount) amt FROM ledger WHERE day>{start} AND day<= {D} GROUP BY category ORDER BY category")
    total=sum((row['amt'] or 0) for row in rows)
    print('total', total)
    for row in rows: print(row)

print('\nSubscription status counts')
for row in q("SELECT status, COUNT(*) n, SUM(seat_count) seats, SUM(effective_price*seat_count) mrr FROM subscriptions GROUP BY status"):
    print(row)

print('\nActive subscribed by group/plan/list/promo')
rows=q("""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.listed_price, s.promotion, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) mrr,
       AVG(? - s.start_day) avg_age
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan, s.listed_price, s.promotion
ORDER BY mrr DESC
""".replace('?',str(D)))
for row in rows: print(row)

print('\nBilling due next 7/14/30/63 days active (billing mod current+1..h)')
# Billing day condition: daily billing every 30 days on day %30 = billing_day_mod30? Need next h days >D<=D+h.
for h in [7,14,30,63]:
    # Use modulo condition enumerate days
    mods=tuple(sorted({(D+i)%30 for i in range(1,h+1)}))
    modlist=','.join(map(str,mods))
    rows=q(f"""
    SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.listed_price, s.promotion, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) mrr
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({modlist})
    GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,s.listed_price,s.promotion
    ORDER BY mrr DESC LIMIT 30
    """)
    print('\nhorizon',h,'mods',mods)
    total=q(f"SELECT COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) mrr FROM subscriptions s WHERE status='subscribed' AND billing_day_mod30 IN ({modlist})")[0]
    print('TOTAL', total)
    for row in rows: print(row)

print('\nCancellations last 7 days by group/plan/list/promo age')
rows=q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.listed_price, s.promotion, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, AVG({D}-s.start_day) avg_age
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day>{D-7} AND s.end_day<={D}
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,s.listed_price,s.promotion
ORDER BY n DESC
""")
for row in rows: print(row)

print('\nChurn/renewal outcome for due mods last 7? subscriptions ended/active with billing_mod in previous week and start<end')
mods=tuple(sorted({(D-7+i)%30 for i in range(1,8)}))  # days 428-434? D=434, prev week days 428..434 mods 8..14? yes
modlist=','.join(map(str,mods))
rows=q(f"""
WITH base AS (
 SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.listed_price, s.promotion, s.status, s.end_day, s.start_day, s.billing_day_mod30, s.seat_count
 FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
 WHERE s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({modlist}) AND c.customer_type='small'
)
SELECT group_id, plan, listed_price, promotion,
 SUM(CASE WHEN status='subscribed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) active_now,
 SUM(CASE WHEN status='cancelled' AND end_day>{D-7} AND end_day<={D} THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) cancelled_week,
 COUNT(*) total_rows,
 SUM(CASE WHEN status='subscribed' THEN effective_price ELSE 0 END) dummy
FROM (
 SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.listed_price, s.promotion, s.status, s.end_day, s.start_day, s.billing_day_mod30, s.seat_count, s.effective_price
 FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
 WHERE s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({modlist}) AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.start_day <= {D-30}
) s
GROUP BY group_id, plan, listed_price, promotion
ORDER BY (cancelled_week+active_now) DESC LIMIT 50
""")
for row in rows: print(row)

print('\nOpen issues')
for row in q(f"SELECT group_id, status, days_open, COUNT(*) n FROM issues WHERE status='open' GROUP BY group_id,status,days_open ORDER BY group_id,days_open"):
    print(row)
print('\nResolved issues last 7 by age')
for row in q(f"SELECT group_id, days_open, COUNT(*) n FROM issues WHERE status='resolved' AND resolved_day>{D-7} AND resolved_day<={D} GROUP BY group_id,days_open ORDER BY group_id,days_open"):
    print(row)

print('\nUsage/compute ledger by day last 10')
for row in q(f"SELECT day, category, SUM(amount) amt, COUNT(*) n FROM ledger WHERE day>{D-10} AND day<={D} AND category IN ('compute','capacity','operations','development','advertising','subscription_payment','lead_acquisition_cost') GROUP BY day,category ORDER BY day,category"):
    print(row)

print('\nEnterprise latest non-agent open threads count and seats by group/type')
rows=q("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT c.group_id, t.thread_type, t.sender, COUNT(*) n, SUM(t.seat_count) seats, MIN(t.day) min_day, MAX(t.day) max_day
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns t ON t.message_id=l.mid JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=t.customer_id
WHERE t.sender!='agent'
GROUP BY c.group_id,t.thread_type,t.sender
ORDER BY seats DESC
""")
for row in rows: print(row)

print('\nEnterprise latest customer sample')
rows=q("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT t.message_id,t.customer_id,c.group_id,t.thread_type,t.turn_number,t.sender,t.day,t.seat_count,substr(t.message_text,1,180) msg,t.offer_json
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns t ON t.message_id=l.mid JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=t.customer_id
WHERE t.sender!='agent'
ORDER BY t.seat_count DESC LIMIT 20
""")
for row in rows: print(row)

print('\nActive enterprise subs')
for row in q("""
SELECT c.customer_id,c.group_id,s.plan,s.listed_price,s.effective_price,s.seat_count,s.start_day,s.billing_day_mod30 FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='subscribed'
"""):
    print(row)

print('\nRecent social posts')
try:
    posts=nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=14, limit=50)
    pprint(posts)
except Exception as e: print('social err',e)

print('\nMarket overview')
try:
    pprint(nm.market.get_market_overview())
except Exception as e: print('market err',e)

print('\nResearch projects')
try:
    pprint(nm.research.list_research_projects())
except Exception as e: print('research err',e)

import novamind_api as nm
D=nm.vars.current_day
# Select all open enterprise threads whose latest message is non-agent (must reply) OR old cheap offers sent on day 427 (latest agent) to supersede with profitable A-only offers.
rows=nm.query(f"""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
base AS (
 SELECT t.customer_id,c.group_id,t.sender,t.day,t.thread_type
 FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns t ON t.message_id=l.mid JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=t.customer_id
 WHERE t.closed=0 AND t.thread_type='new_lead' AND c.customer_type='large'
)
SELECT customer_id, group_id, sender, day, thread_type FROM base
WHERE sender!='agent' OR (sender='agent' AND day=427)
ORDER BY group_id, customer_id
""")['rows']
prices={'E3':89.0,'D_E02':59.0,'D_E07':119.0}
deals=[]; skipped=[]
for r in rows:
    p=prices.get(r['group_id'])
    if p is None:
        skipped.append(r)
    else:
        deals.append([r['customer_id'], [['A', p]]])
print('D',D,'selected',len(rows),'deals',len(deals),'skipped',len(skipped))
from collections import Counter
print('by_group',Counter(r['group_id'] for r in rows))
print('by_sender',Counter(r['sender'] for r in rows))
# send in chunks to avoid payload limits
all_results=[]
for i in range(0,len(deals),100):
    chunk=deals[i:i+100]
    res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=chunk)
    results=res.get('results',[])
    print('chunk',i//100,'sent',len(chunk),'results',len(results),'successes',sum(1 for x in results if x.get('success')))
    # print failures
    for x in results:
        if not x.get('success'):
            print('FAIL',x)
    all_results.extend(results)
print('TOTAL success',sum(1 for x in all_results if x.get('success')),'of',len(all_results))
# verify latest non-agent remains
verify=nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT c.group_id,t.sender,t.thread_type,COUNT(*) n,SUM(t.seat_count) seats,MIN(t.day) min_day,MAX(t.day) max_day
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns t ON t.message_id=l.mid JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=t.customer_id
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND t.sender!='agent'
GROUP BY c.group_id,t.sender,t.thread_type ORDER BY n DESC
""")['rows']
print('latest non-agent open after:',verify)
# verify latest agent by group/day counts
verify2=nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT c.group_id,t.sender,t.thread_type,COUNT(*) n,MIN(t.day) min_day,MAX(t.day) max_day
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns t ON t.message_id=l.mid JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=t.customer_id
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND t.sender='agent'
GROUP BY c.group_id,t.sender,t.thread_type ORDER BY n DESC
""")['rows']
print('latest agent open after:',verify2[:10])

import novamind_api as nm
D=nm.vars.current_day
rows=nm.query(f"""
WITH offered AS (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM enterprise_turns WHERE sender='agent' AND day={D-7}),
latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
sys AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE sender!='agent' GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT t.customer_id,c.group_id,sysmsg.seat_count seats,t.day,t.sender
FROM offered o JOIN latest l ON l.customer_id=o.customer_id JOIN enterprise_turns t ON t.message_id=l.mid
JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=t.customer_id
JOIN sys ON sys.customer_id=t.customer_id JOIN enterprise_turns sysmsg ON sysmsg.message_id=sys.mid
WHERE t.sender='agent' AND t.day={D-7} AND c.group_id IN ('E3','D_E02','D_E07')
ORDER BY c.group_id,t.customer_id
""")['rows']
print('old cheap remaining',len(rows))
from collections import Counter
print('groups',Counter(r['group_id'] for r in rows),'seats',sum(r['seats'] for r in rows))
if rows:
    deals=[{'customer_id': r['customer_id']} for r in rows]
    allres=[]
    for i in range(0,len(deals),100):
        res=nm.enterprise.reject_enterprise_deal(deals=deals[i:i+100])
        results=res.get('results',[])
        print('chunk',i//100,'success',sum(1 for x in results if x.get('success')),'of',len(results))
        for x in results:
            if not x.get('success'):
                print('FAIL',x)
        allres+=results
    print('total rejected successes',sum(1 for x in allres if x.get('success')))
# verify
print(nm.query(f"""
WITH offered AS (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM enterprise_turns WHERE sender='agent' AND day={D-7}),
latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT c.group_id,t.sender,t.day,COUNT(*) n FROM offered o JOIN latest l ON l.customer_id=o.customer_id JOIN enterprise_turns t ON t.message_id=l.mid JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=t.customer_id
WHERE c.group_id IN ('E3','D_E02','D_E07')
GROUP BY c.group_id,t.sender,t.day ORDER BY c.group_id,t.day
""")['rows'])

 MAX(u.usage_units) max_day,
 SUM(CASE WHEN u.usage_units>1000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) days_gt1k,
 SUM(CASE WHEN u.usage_units>5000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) days_gt5k,
 SUM(CASE WHEN u.usage_units>10000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) days_gt10k
FROM daily_usage u JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=u.customer_id JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=u.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'

"""):
 print(row)

import novamind_api as nm
from pprint import pprint
D=nm.vars.current_day

def q(sql):
 r=nm.query(sql)
 if r.get('warning'): print('WARNING', r['warning'])
 return r['rows']
print('DAY',D)
# Active subscriber issue summary
print('\nActive subscriber open issues by group/age buckets')
rows=q(f"""
SELECT i.group_id,
 SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open=0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) age0,
 SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open BETWEEN 1 AND 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) age1_2,
 SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open BETWEEN 3 AND 7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) age3_7,
 SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open >7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) age8p,
 COUNT(*) total
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id ORDER BY total DESC
""")
for row in rows: print(row)
print('\nActive subscriber open issue count direct', q("""SELECT COUNT(*) n FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed' WHERE i.status='open'""")[0])

# Churn due last week only active before billing? Estimate cancellations / (payments + cancellations for due mods) by current/old cohorts
print('\nLast week payments by group/plan/list/promo (ledger note parse?) sample notes')
for row in q(f"SELECT day, amount, note FROM ledger WHERE day>{D-7} AND day<= {D} AND category='subscription_payment' LIMIT 10"):
 print(row)

# Payment amounts distribution last 7
print('\nPayment amount distribution last 7')
for row in q(f"SELECT amount, COUNT(*) n, SUM(amount) total FROM ledger WHERE day>{D-7} AND day<= {D} AND category='subscription_payment' GROUP BY amount ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 30"):
 print(row)

# Current active by billing mod next 63 with projected next charges after current promo config? Use current row only; need due per week
print('\nDue weekly active current mrr by week')
for wk in range(1,10):
 days=range(D+7*(wk-1)+1, D+7*wk+1)
 mods=tuple(sorted({d%30 for d in days}))
 ml=','.join(map(str,mods))
 row=q(f"SELECT COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) current_mrr FROM subscriptions s WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({ml})")[0]
 print(wk, list(days)[0], list(days)[-1], mods, row)

# Active cohorts by billing_mod, plan,list,promo start_day bucket for next week due
mods=tuple(sorted({(D+i)%30 for i in range(1,8)})); ml=','.join(map(str,mods))
print('\nNext week due cohorts by age bucket')
for row in q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.listed_price, s.promotion,
 CASE WHEN {D}-s.start_day < 60 THEN '<60' WHEN {D}-s.start_day < 180 THEN '60-179' WHEN {D}-s.start_day < 300 THEN '180-299' WHEN {D}-s.start_day < 360 THEN '300-359' ELSE '360+' END age_bucket,
 COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({ml})
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,s.listed_price,s.promotion,age_bucket
ORDER BY mrr DESC LIMIT 50
"""):
 print(row)

# Enterprise outcomes for week61 offers (agent messages days 421-427)
print('\nEnterprise agent sends last 2 weeks by day/count/offers sample')
for row in q(f"SELECT day, COUNT(*) n, SUM(seat_count) seats FROM enterprise_turns WHERE sender='agent' AND day>{D-14} AND day<= {D} GROUP BY day ORDER BY day"):
 print(row)
print('\nEnterprise accepted/closed last 14')
for row in q(f"SELECT t.day,t.close_reason,c.group_id,t.thread_type,COUNT(*) n,SUM(t.seat_count) seats FROM enterprise_turns t JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=t.customer_id WHERE t.closed=1 AND t.day>{D-14} AND t.day<= {D} GROUP BY t.day,t.close_reason,c.group_id,t.thread_type ORDER BY t.day"):
 print(row)
print('\nEnterprise latest agent open from last week offers summary')
for row in q(f"""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT c.group_id,t.thread_type,t.sender,COUNT(*) n,SUM(t.seat_count) seats,MIN(t.day) min_day,MAX(t.day) max_day
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns t ON t.message_id=l.mid JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=t.customer_id
WHERE t.sender='agent' AND t.day>{D-14}
GROUP BY c.group_id,t.thread_type,t.sender
ORDER BY seats DESC
"""):
 print(row)

FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns t ON t.message_id=l.mid JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=t.customer_id
